title: potentilla recta var. laciniosa
intro: Potentilla recta var. laciniosa, also known as the five-finger cinquefoil, is a variety of the perennial flowering plant Potentilla recta, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Potentilla recta var. laciniosa are numerous, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to support the immune system and combat oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used in salves and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
